summary: Frontend visual/regression test of /admin/outreach/activity after restyle to grid-based Leaderboard-style table. All 8 acceptance criteria pass on both desktop (1920x900) and mobile (390x844). Tables render correctly, mobile hides Category+Posted columns, no horizontal overflow, rows are clickable anchors opening x.com in a new tab, ExternalLink icon replaces draft tweet text.
critical_code_review_comments: Minor: `fmtDate` silently falls back to `s.slice(0,10)` for unparseable dates which could expose raw ISO-like strings — acceptable but consider '—'.
Minor: `useBreakpoints` re-renders both table components on every resize; for 4 rows it's fine, but a `matchMedia` listener would be cheaper.
test_credentials: admin password: papersumo2025 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Token obtained via POST /api/admin/login and stored in localStorage+sessionStorage under key 'admin_token'.
seed_data_creation: None — used existing backend data (4 quotes, 1 like).
context_for_next_testing_agent: OutreachActivityPage.jsx now uses a CSS grid layout (gridTemplateColumns dynamically computed per breakpoint) mirroring LeaderboardTable. Columns on desktop quotes: [#, Paper, Quoted, Category, Posted, Tweet]; mobile quotes: [#, Paper, Quoted, Tweet]. Likes desktop: [#, Paper, Liked, Liked at, Tweet]; mobile likes: [#, Paper, Liked, Tweet]. Each row is an <a target=_blank> pointing to quote_tweet_url (quotes) or tweet_url (likes) with stopPropagation on the inner handle badge. data-testid='activity-quotes-table' / 'activity-likes-table' render conditionally with the active tab. data-testid for rows: activity-quote-<handle> / activity-like-<handle>. Search filters by paper title, handle, arxiv id, and authors.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — this was a visual regression check, not a bug fix. All 8 acceptance criteria from the review request verified green.
